skill.name: benchling-integration
skill.description: Integrate with the Benchling R&D platform when you need to programmatically manage registry entities, inventory, ELN entries, workflows, events, or data warehouse analytics via API/SDK.
skill.summary: Programmatic integration with Benchling R&D platform for registry, inventory, and ELN management via Python SDK and REST API.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only reference skill with no executable scripts. The static analyzer found 455 potential issues, but all detected patterns are in Markdown code blocks within reference documentation (API endpoint docs, authentication guide, SDK reference). Code examples use placeholder values and the documentation explicitly encourages security best practices including environment variable usage and secrets management. No malicious intent or actual security risk was identified. All findings are false positives arising from the analyzer matching patterns in documentation code examples rather than executable code.

low_findings.title: Documentation-only code examples flagged as external_commands
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 325 instances of Ruby/shell backtick execution patterns detected across all 4 markdown files. All instances are within Markdown code block fences showing CLI commands (curl, pip, poetry) and Python SDK usage examples. The skill explicitly states 'No packaged executable script was detected.' These are reference code examples, not executed code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All patterns are in Markdown code blocks. The skill has no executable scripts. Statement 'No packaged executable script was detected' confirms this is documentation-only.

low_findings.title: Documentation examples using os.environ flagged as env_access
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 50 instances of environment variable access detected. All are legitimate documentation examples showing proper credential management using os.environ['BENCHLING_API_KEY']. The documentation explicitly warns against hardcoding credentials and recommends secrets managers. Values use placeholders like 'your_api_key'.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern appears in code examples with explicit security warnings. Placeholder values used throughout. Documentation follows industry best practices for credential storage.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs and fetch examples flagged as network
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: 42 instances of network-related patterns including hardcoded URLs and fetch API calls. All are in documentation showing Benchling API endpoints (https://{tenant}.benchling.com), JavaScript error handling examples, and resource links. These are legitimate reference URLs for the Benchling platform API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to the Benchling platform. Fetch calls are JavaScript documentation examples. This is expected behavior for an API integration reference skill.

content.user_title: Manage Benchling R&D data through API integration
content.value_statement: This skill helps users programmatically interact with the Benchling R&D platform using the Python SDK and REST API. It eliminates manual data entry by providing ready-to-use code patterns for registry, inventory, ELN, and workflow operations.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage registry entities including DNA, RNA, and amino acid sequences through the Benchling SDK
Automate inventory operations such as container transfers, box creation, and location management
Create and update ELN notebook entries with custom schema fields and entity links
Manage workflow tasks including creation, status updates, and async task monitoring with polling
Build event-driven integrations using Benchling events and AWS EventBridge for real-time pipelines
Query the Benchling Data Warehouse for analytics and reporting using SQL access patterns
limitations: Requires valid Benchling API credentials with appropriate tenant permissions
Cannot access data outside the user's Benchling tenant access boundaries
Does not include GUI automation, browser interactions, or UI-level operations
No packaged executable scripts are included; the skill provides reference code and documentation

use_cases.title: Automated DNA sequence registration
use_cases.description: A lab automation engineer needs to register hundreds of DNA sequences from a high-throughput cloning pipeline. This skill provides SDK code patterns to create, validate, and organize sequences in the Benchling Registry with custom schema fields.

use_cases.title: ELN experiment documentation
use_cases.description: A research scientist wants to programmatically create structured ELN entries with custom fields linking experiment results to registered entities. This skill provides the SDK patterns to automate notebook documentation.

use_cases.title: Inventory tracking automation
use_cases.description: A lab manager needs to track sample locations, transfers, and check-in/check-out operations across multiple freezers. This skill enables automated inventory management through the Benchling API and SDK.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Benchling DNA sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to create a new DNA sequence in Benchling. Use the benchling-sdk to create a DnaSequence with name 'pET28a-GFP', bases 'ATCGATCG', circular structure, and folder ID 'fld_abc123'. Show me the complete Python code using environment variables for authentication.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time Benchling SDK user creating a registry entity

prompt_templates.title: Transfer laboratory containers
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write Python code using the benchling-sdk to transfer containers from one storage location to another. Include error handling for cases where the container is not found and verification that the transfer succeeded.

prompt_templates.title: Build a data sync pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Python script that syncs newly created DNA sequences from an external LIMS system into Benchling Registry. Include pagination handling, error handling, deduplication by name, and structured logging. Use environment variables for all credentials.

prompt_templates.title: Implement event-driven integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to set up a Benchling event-driven integration using AWS EventBridge. Include the event schema format for entity creation events, how to configure event subscriptions, and how to process events in an AWS Lambda function.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cloud architect designing real-time laboratory data pipelines

output_examples.input: Create a DNA sequence in Benchling for my plasmid construct named pCRISPR-001 with bases ATCGATCG in folder fld_abc123.
output_examples.output: Generated Python code using the benchling-sdk. The script imports required classes (Benchling, ApiKeyAuth, DnaSequenceCreate), authenticates using BENCHLING_API_KEY and BENCHLING_TENANT_URL from environment variables, creates a DnaSequence entity with the specified name and bases, and prints the assigned entity ID and name as confirmation.

output_examples.input: List all containers stored in freezer box FRZ-BOX-A1 and show their names, barcodes, and last modified dates.
output_examples.output: Generated Python code that authenticates to Benchling, calls containers.list() filtered by parent_storage_id for the specified box, iterates through paginated results, and displays each container's name, ID, barcode, and modified_at timestamp in a formatted table.

best_practices: Store Benchling API keys and secrets in environment variables or a secrets manager instead of hardcoding them in source files.
Use server-side filters and pagination parameters to minimize data transfer and reduce processing overhead.
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff to handle rate limiting (429 responses) and transient server errors.
anti_patterns: Hardcoding API credentials directly in source code or committing them to version control repositories.
Loading all data without pagination filters and filtering results on the client side instead of the server.
Ignoring rate limit headers and sending too many requests in a short time window without backoff handling.

faq.question: What authentication methods does Benchling support?
faq.answer: Benchling supports API key authentication for personal scripts, OAuth 2.0 client credentials for applications, and OpenID Connect for enterprise SSO integration with identity providers.

faq.question: Do I need to install the Benchling SDK?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the benchling-sdk package using pip or poetry. Python 3.7 or higher is required for SDK usage.

faq.question: Can this skill modify data in my Benchling tenant?
faq.answer: The skill generates code for CRUD operations but only executes what you choose to run. Always review generated code in a development tenant before using it with production data.

faq.question: What is the Benchling Data Warehouse used for?
faq.answer: It provides SQL-based access for analytics and reporting on inventory trends, compliance reports, and data aggregations across your Benchling tenant.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all Benchling entity types?
faq.answer: Yes, the reference documentation covers DNA, RNA, and AA sequences, custom entities, mixtures, containers, boxes, plates, locations, ELN entries, and workflow tasks.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with production data?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation-only and provides code patterns for you to review. Always test generated code in a development Benchling tenant first and follow your organization's security policies.
